label: Mid-2020s America
description: Post-pandemic era. EVs mainstream. Retail apocalypse mature — many vacant big boxes. Delivery culture. LED and screens everywhere. Minimalist branding.

specific_models: 2018-2024 Toyota Camry — aggressive mesh grille, sporty sedan
2020-2025 Tesla Model 3 — minimalist, no grille, flush handles
2020-2025 Tesla Model Y — crossover, closed front, sleek
2019-2025 Toyota RAV4 — boxy rugged crossover, very common
2021-2025 Ford F-150 — refreshed, full-width LED bar options
2022-2025 Ford F-150 Lightning — electric pickup, light bar front
2024-2025 Tesla Cybertruck — angular stainless steel, unmistakable
2020-2025 Hyundai Tucson — sharp parametric grille design
2021-2025 Kia Telluride — boxy upscale three-row SUV
2021-2025 Ford Mustang Mach-E — electric crossover, sleek
visual_characteristics: full-width LED light bars front and rear

specific_models: 2021-2025 Ford F-150 — full-width LED bar, bold

models: 2020-2025 Tesla Model Y — electric crossover, mainstream

fuel.context: EV charging stations widespread, gas prices volatile, EVs roughly 8 percent of new sales

characteristics: vacant former big box stores common
outdoor dining structures from pandemic era
EV charging canopies in parking lots
large convenience stores with fresh food
EV charging stalls added alongside pumps
vacant storefront and ghost signs common
drive-through digital menu boards with AI ordering
signage.typography_style: minimalist geometric sans-serif, heavily simplified flat logos

characteristics: green highway signs with Highway Gothic font

items: smart bus shelters with digital displays and USB charging

characteristics: overhead lines in older areas, underground in new developments

advertising.style: digital, social media native, video-first, minimalist clean design

lighting.street_lights: LED standard everywhere — bright white, smart dimming in some cities
lighting.signs: full color LED digital displays everywhere

photography.grain: very clean, minimal to no noise
photography.style: ultra sharp computational photography, vivid HDR, smartphone social media aesthetic

downtown_street.narrative: A 2025 main street — minimalist flat-logo storefronts, QR codes on the glass, EVs at the curb, delivery culture in plain view.
storefronts: the old video store space now a specialty coffee bar with a minimalist flat logo
the same diner, still going, 'COFFEE $3.50' shown on a small digital menu screen in the window
barber shop with its spinning barber pole — six decades on
boba tea shop with pastel minimalist branding and a QR-code ordering decal
nail and lash studio with a pink LED script sign
delivery-only ghost kitchen storefront with pickup shelves visible through the glass
boutique fitness studio with black-and-white minimalist branding
plant and gift shop with a hand-lettered kraft-paper window display
window_signs: ORDER AHEAD — SCAN THE QR CODE
BOBA — BUY ONE GET ONE FRIDAYS
people_activities: a delivery courier with an insulated backpack picking up an order
a woman in athleisure carrying a boba tea
several people looking at smartphones as they walk
a person gliding past on an electric scooter
a couple filming a vertical video on a phone gimbal
sidewalk diners in a parklet dining structure
a barista handing an order to a rideshare driver at the curb
a man in an oversized tee and chunky sneakers walking a small dog
an older couple reading the diner's digital menu in the window
extras: the ghost-sign wall now a commissioned mural with bold flat graphics
a shared e-scooter and e-bike dock at the curb
a parklet outdoor dining structure occupying two parking spaces
a stack of delivery packages waiting at a shop door

gas_station.narrative: A 2025 fuel stop — LED canopy, full-color digital price sign, EV chargers beside the pumps, and a convenience store selling fresh food.
storefronts: modern canopy with bright white LED lighting
full-color digital price sign on the road pole
pay-at-pump screens playing video ads
EV fast-charging stalls alongside the gas pumps
large convenience store with 'FRESH FOOD — MADE TO ORDER' branding
drive-through coffee lane attached to the store
free air and vacuum station at the lot edge
people_activities: a driver self-serving at the pump
an EV owner plugged in at the charging stall, looking at a phone
a delivery van driver walking out with a coffee
a customer tapping a phone to pay at the pump
a clerk restocking the grab-and-go cooler behind the glass
a driver watching the ad screen while fueling
extras: digital menu screens glowing in the store windows
stacked bags of ice and firewood outside

strip_mall.narrative: A 2025 strip mall rebuilt around services and food — nail studios, phone stores, and pickup-driven restaurants where retail used to be.
storefronts: former big retail unit converted to a trampoline park and gym with a bold flat logo
boba tea franchise with pastel signage and QR decals on the glass
nail and spa studio with an LED script sign
wireless carrier store with a backlit white interior
vape and smoke shop with LED window signs
dollar store anchor with green signage
urgent care clinic with red-and-white minimal branding
delivery ghost kitchen with 'DRIVER PICKUP ONLY' parking signs
people_activities: a courier loading insulated delivery bags into a hatchback
a woman in athleisure carrying a yoga mat
a man scrolling his phone outside the nail studio
a family heading into the trampoline park
a person carrying a tray of boba teas to a car
a curbside-pickup attendant bringing bags to a parked SUV
extras: a pair of EV chargers at the corner of the lot
an e-scooter parked by the walkway

default.narrative: An ordinary 2025 American commercial street — flat minimalist logos, QR codes, EVs and delivery drivers.
storefronts: local diner with 'COFFEE $3.50' on a digital menu screen
barber shop with a spinning barber pole
specialty coffee bar in a former video store space
boba tea shop with pastel branding
nail and lash studio with an LED script sign
ghost kitchen storefront with pickup shelves
people_activities: a delivery courier with an insulated backpack
several people looking at smartphones
a person on an electric scooter
a woman in athleisure with a boba tea
a couple filming a vertical video
a rideshare driver picking up an order at the curb
extras: a mural where the old ghost-sign used to be
an e-scooter dock at the curb
packages stacked by a shop door
